== Notes for [[Item:Q77813|Q77813]] ==

Anicia Juliana (Constantinople, 462 - 527/528) was a Roman imperial princess, the daughter of the Western Roman Emperor Olybrius, of the Anicii, by Placidia the younger daughter of Emperor Valentinian III and Licinia Eudoxia.

She married Flavius Areobindus Dagalaiphus Areobindus, and their children included Olybrius, consul for 491. With her husband, she spent her life at the pre-Justinian court of Constantinople, of which she was considered "both the most aristocratic and the wealthiest inhabitant".

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anicia_Juliana

SOURCE NOTES:
http://www.american-pictures.com/genealogy/persons/per04530.htm#0

She was the first patron of the arts who wasn't a royal per recorded history.
She influenced the artists she commissioned by determining the content and the style of the art.
Commissioned the famous Juliana Anicia Codex.
Comissioned and donated the basilica of Saint Polyeuctus.

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
